text.title: ads-meta-campaign-setup Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ads-meta-campaign-setup as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Running campaigns without a verified Meta Pixel.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: No conversion data means the delivery system cannot optimize — ads serve to random segments at maximum CPM with zero feedback loop.

failure-mode.description: Choosing Traffic objective when the actual goal is leads or purchases.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Traffic optimizes for cheap clicks; it finds audiences that click but don't convert, inflating CTR while producing zero revenue.

failure-mode.description: Using an audience under 100K for interest-based prospecting.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Audience exhaustion occurs within days; high frequency causes CPM inflation and ad fatigue before the algorithm can learn anything meaningful.

failure-mode.description: Launching with one ad per ad set.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: No creative variation means no optimization path — Meta cannot identify a winner and the campaign performance is limited to whatever the single creative happens to achieve.

failure-mode.description: Editing budgets, targeting, or bids during the first 3-7 days.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Resets the learning phase counter; the campaign re-enters unstable high-CPA delivery, negating any potential improvement from the edit.

failure-mode.description: Omitting UTM parameters from destination URLs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without UTMs, GA4 attributes Meta-driven sessions as direct traffic, making cross-channel ROAS comparison impossible.
